title: brachypodium sylvaticum subsp. pubescens
intro: Brachypodium sylvaticum subsp. pubescens, commonly known as hairy false brome, is a subspecies of the Brachypodium sylvaticum species, a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of hairy false brome include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
